useCases.title: Transpareo - product truth anyone can verify
useCases.summary:
Every product makes claims - recycled, sustainable, ethically sourced, repairable - and today a shopper has no way to check any of them. Under the EU's new Digital Product Passport rules, every product will soon carry a digital record reachable from a QR code. But a record you cannot verify is just marketing with extra steps.

Transpareo makes that record a verifiable credential. Each product passport is sealed with a W3C Data Integrity proof over Ed25519 and bound to the maker's own domain through a did:web identifier. There is no wallet app to install - the passport carries itself. Scan the QR code and our open-source viewer, the Transpareo Time Machine, verifies the signature right in the browser, offline, against nothing but a public key. You are not trusting the brand, and you are not trusting us. You are checking the maths.

The impact is social: shoppers can tell real sustainability claims from marketing, repairers and recyclers get data they can trust, and regulators verify independently. Every version is kept in a foundation-owned write-once archive, so the record outlives its issuer. Live demo: https://time-machine.transpareo.com/
useCases.userJourney:
1. A maker (manufacturer or importer) creates a product passport in Transpareo and publishes it. Transpareo seals that version as a verifiable credential: a W3C Data Integrity proof over Ed25519, signed with a key bound to the maker's own domain via a did:web identifier. A GS1 Digital Link QR code on the product points to the passport.

2. A shopper scans the QR code with their phone. Nothing is installed, no wallet, no account: the link opens our open-source viewer, the Transpareo Time Machine, in the browser.

3. The viewer fetches the passport and its proof, resolves the maker's public key from the did:web identifier, and verifies the signature client-side, offline, against that key. A pass means the record is authentic and unaltered, and the shopper can verify every earlier version too.

4. A repairer or recycler later scans the same code to read repair and material data, and verifies it the same way.

5. A market-surveillance authority verifies the same passport independently through a dedicated Authority API, which logs an audit entry on every access. Each party checks from its own side, trusting the maths rather than the maker or Transpareo.
